request.question: What was the second album by the singer who was a client of Andros Rodriguez, and whose debut album was  "Santogold"?

title: Santigold discography
The Explorers Club (band)
EggHunt Records
Andros Rodriguez
Against All Will
Santigold
Noah Georgeson
The Shortwave Set
Sarah Perrotta
Richard Davies (musician)
sentences: Santi White, better known by her stage name Santigold (formerly Sant"o"gold, a stage name under which most of her material has been released), is an American songwriter, producer, and singer.
White's discography under the name Santogold consists of her self-titled debut album, "Santogold", and four promotional singles, a mixtape, and four music videos.
All of these were released in 2008, except the single "Creator", which was released in late 2007.
"Santogold" peaked at number 74 on the "Billboard" 200, and reached numbers 2 and 6 on "Billboard"' s Dance/Electronic Albums and Independent Albums, respectively.
The album also charted in Belgium, France, the Netherlands, and the United Kingdom.
The Explorers Club are a Pop rock band originally from the coast of South Carolina.
They are a 5-man band whose debut album was released by Dead Oceans.
The music of their first album is heavily influenced by the vocal harmony styles and production of The Beach Boys.
They are also influenced by classic rock and roll arrangements as made popular by The Beatles, Chuck Berry, Phil Spector, The Zombies, The Monkees and The Byrds.
Their second album shows more influences of soft-pop artists of the early-1970s such as Burt Bacharach, Glen Campbell, etc.
The band relocated to Nashville, TN in 2014 and released their third studio album Together on June 24, 2016
EggHunt Records is an independent record label based in Richmond, Virginia.
The label was founded by Adam Henceroth and Gregory Gendron in spring of 2014.
EggHunt Records focuses on upcoming, independent artists and releases material on cassette, CD, and vinyl physical formats, as well as digital download and streaming online.
EggHunt Records maintains an active roster of artists who have toured with notable acts such as The War on Drugs and Houndmouth.
EggHunt Records alumni include Lucy Dacus, whose debut album, "No Burden", was released on EggHunt Records in 2016.
In 2017, EggHunt Records signed Eric Slick of Dr. Dog to release his solo debut album, "Palisades".
Andros Rodriguez is a multi-platinum, Grammy award-winning music producer, engineer and mixer.

His diverse catalog of clients include Pharrell, Justin Timberlake, Christina Aguilera, Florence + the Machine, Shakira, Bleachers, Whitney Houston, Cobra Starship, Santigold, Jewel, Lenny Kravitz, Kelly Clarkson, Girls' Generation, Patti Labelle, James Blunt, Ludacris, and Huang Zitao.

Against All Will is a rock quartet from Los Angeles, California, whose debut album lineup was formed in Spring 2009 by Jimmy Allen (formerly of Puddle of Mudd), Jeff Current (formerly of Seven Story Drop), Steve "Boomstick" Wilson (formerly of Dead Kennedys and t.A.T.u.), and Cello Dias (formerly of Soulfly).
Songs "All About You" and "The Drug I Need" from their debut album entitled A Rhyme & Reason ranked in the national rock radio top 50 in 2010.
Santi White (born September 25, 1976), better known by her stage name Santigold (formerly Santogold), is an American singer and producer.
Her debut album, "Santogold", including the singles "Creator" and "L.E.S. Artistes", received very favorable reviews in 2008.
Her second album, "Master of My Make-Believe", was released in 2012.
In February 2016, she released her third album "99¢".
Noah Georgeson is a Grammy winning musician, producer, and solo recording artist.
Georgeson's debut album "Find Shelter" was released through Plain Recordings on November 28, 2006.
Born in San Anselmo, California, he moved with his family to Nevada City, California at the age of three.

Georgeson studied classical guitar and music composition, receiving his BA in composition from San Francisco State University in 2001, and, with a recommendation from Terry Riley, he attended Mills College, receiving his Master of Fine Arts in 2003.
While at Mills, Georgeson studied with Fred Frith, Pauline Oliveros, and Alvin Curran.
Georgeson first found popular success as a part of San Francisco band The Pleased, along with fellow member Joanna Newsom, whose debut album "The Milk-Eyed Mender" he produced.

As a musician, producer, and mixer, Georgeson has since worked with Devendra Banhart, The Strokes, Little Joy, Bert Jansch, Charlotte Gainsbourg, Robin Pecknold, Mason Jennings, Cedric Bixler, Adam Green, Os Mutantes, Adan Jodorowsky, Harper Simon, Flo Morrissey, Cate Le Bon, and Rodrigo Amarante.
The Shortwave Set are a British alternative pop band whose debut album, "The Debt Collection", was released in 2005 on Independiente Records.
The band's music combines sample-based music with more traditional songwriting and instrumentation to create a sound the group have described as "Victorian Funk".
The band's second album, "Replica Sun Machine", was released on 12 May 2008 by Wall of Sound.
Sarah Perrotta (born 1979, United States) is a Hudson Valley based singer, songwriter and pianist.
Perrotta was the front woman of the indie-rock band Outloud Dreamer whose debut album “Drink The Sky” was named best modern rock album of 2000 by WKZE Radio 98.1FM.
Perrotta’s second album “The Well” was self-released in 2008 and features Tony Levin on bass and Garth Hudson on accordion.
The album was mixed by Grammy Award winner Malcolm Burn.
Her most recent record is entitled tonight released under her surname.
Richard Davies is an Australian-American musician.
He first came to prominence in the early 1990s as leader of the Australian band The Moles.
Upon moving to the United States, Davies joined with Eric Matthews to form Cardinal, whose debut album, "Cardinal," was released by Flydaddy in 1994.
He has since released several solo albums including "There's Never Been a Crowd Like This" (1996), "Telegraph" (1998), and "Barbarians" (2000).
In 2009 Davies and Guided by Voices front man Robert Pollard, collaborating under the name Cosmos, released an album, "Jar of Jam / Ton of Bricks."
In 2012 Davies and Matthews reformed Cardinal and released a second album, "Hymns."

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

You are the first-hop summarizer. Compress the supplied passages into a short, actionable intermediate state that lets the next agent know exactly what is present in the passages, what is missing, and what to fetch next. Produce a single `summary` string only.

Summary content requirements (concise: a few short sentences or clauses):
- First-hop evidence: one short paraphrase or a very brief quoted phrase from the passages that links the relevant entities or facts supporting any partial answer. Do not copy long verbatim spans.
- Bridge entity: explicitly name the person/entity mentioned in the passages that the second hop should investigate (use the full name or label exactly as it appears in the passages). If the question uses only a given name and the passages do not disambiguate which individual is meant, state clearly that the identity is missing/ambiguous.
- Missing fact: state in one short sentence which specific fact is required to answer the question (for example: winner name, year, birthplace, or another identifying detail) and confirm it is not present in the given passages.
- Retrieval directions: give one concise, ordered, actionable next-hop plan (1–2 steps). Prefer concrete target pages or query patterns, e.g. "open the named person's biography to check for [missing fact]" or a short query pattern such as '"<NAME>" birthplace OR "born in" site:wikipedia.org' or 'open a chronological list of <AWARD> winners and search for <NAME>'. Do not instruct the system to execute retrieval here — only specify what the next retrieval should fetch.
- Clarification: if ambiguity persists (e.g., only a given name is provided and multiple candidates are possible), include a short clarifying request asking the user to provide the full name.

Hard constraints and tone:
- Be concise and factual. Keep the same neutral, summary tone as before.
- Do not guess, infer, or assert any facts not present in the passages (no candidate answers, no years, no birthplace assertions, etc.).
- Do not ask the user for permission to search external sources. Give explicit next-hop retrieval directions instead.
- Output only the single `summary` field text complying with the above requirements.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict: Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

You are integrating newly retrieved passages (second‑hop evidence) with the first‑hop context to produce a grounded, post‑retrieval summary that preserves the facts the final answer generator will need. Produce a single field `summary` whose value is 1–3 concise sentences that: (1) directly state the answer to the question (e.g., where the studios/recordings were located) based on the available evidence; (2) if any passage explicitly names a production facility or studio and ties that facility to a specific locality (for example by giving the facility name together with a street, town, or county), include one short supporting sentence that paraphrases that passage to show the link between the facility and the locality; and (3) include one very short sentence or clause that synthesizes the context and the supporting passage to place the studios in the stated locality. Paraphrase supporting passages; do not include long verbatim quotations.

If the passages do not conclusively link a named studio to a specific locality, preserve and state that uncertainty rather than asserting unsupported specifics. Avoid headers, meta commentary, long quotations, extra sections, or inventing details not present in the provided context/passages. Keep phrasing compact, factual, and focused on the evidence needed to support the final answer.

Output contract: produce only the `summary` field (1–3 concise sentences) that integrates the first‑hop context with the second‑hop passages as described above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, and `summary_2`, produce the field `answer` as follows. Use only the visible summaries (summary_1 and summary_2) as the evidential basis for the final answer; do not add external facts except where the instructions below explicitly allow a limited, well‑justified use of internal background knowledge. Always produce the final result inside the [[ ## answer ## ]] block and make that block contain only the minimal answer string required by these rules.

1) General minimal-answer rule
- If the summaries unambiguously support a single short answer (a short name, title, demonym, year, or single noun/phrase of roughly 1–5 words), emit only that minimal token sequence as the answer. Normalize the chosen substring by removing any leading/trailing whitespace or surrounding punctuation but preserve the phrase's internal characters and capitalization exactly as shown in the summaries. Do not add quotes, em dashes, braces, labels, trailing punctuation, explanatory clauses, or any additional text.

2) When both summaries agree on an identical short phrase
- If summary_1 and summary_2 contain the same short named-entity string (after lowercasing only for agreement detection), extract that common phrase, strip surrounding punctuation/whitespace, and emit it verbatim (preserving original capitalization) as the sole content of [[ ## answer ## ]] with no surrounding or appended characters.

3) Patterned question triggers (minimal formatting + deterministic selection)
- 'Which ... is from X, A or B?': When the question asks which of two named options is from a place and the summaries clearly indicate a single band/person as the answer, emit only the minimal band/person name supported by the summaries.
- Comparative membership questions (e.g., 'Does A or B have more band members?'): If the summaries clearly indicate which band has more members, emit only that band's name.
- Short common-name questions (e.g., 'What do people in the UK commonly call the location ...?'): If both summaries provide the same short common name, emit only that short name string.

4) Nationality questions
- If the question asks for a nationality/demonym and either summary_1 or summary_2 contains a demonym, emit only the minimal demonym token/phrase. Prefer the first demonym found in summary_1; if none appears in summary_1, use the first found in summary_2. Emit it verbatim (no added words or punctuation).

5) Two-performer composition specificity
- If the question asks for the "type of musical composition" used by a named act and both summaries use a generic word like 'song(s)', you may consult internal knowledge only to determine whether the act is a two-person (duo) group. If you can reliably confirm the act is a two-person group, replace the generic term with the single-word or very short noun that names a composition intended for two performers (for example, 'duet') and emit that single word as the minimal answer. If you cannot reliably confirm duo membership, emit the minimal noun exactly as it appears in the summaries.

6) Narrow assumed referent (single-name trigger)
- If the question supplies only a given name (e.g., 'Maurice') and the visible summaries explicitly mention a specific example with that given name (e.g., a named example appears in the summaries) and the summaries identify the requested fact (e.g., the award), then you may assume that referent and emit the minimal answer (for example, a single year) if you have sufficient internal confidence. If you do not have sufficient internal confidence, follow the normal clarification behavior (emit a brief clarifying question as the answer field) rather than guessing.

7) What to avoid (formatting and content prohibitions)
- Never add explanatory text, full sentences, comparisons, justifications, or provenance in the answer field. Do not append decorative punctuation (em dashes, extra commas, trailing periods), quotes, braces, JSON-like structures, leading newlines, or any characters not part of the normalized minimal phrase. Do not invent facts not present in the summaries except where the two narrowly scoped rules above permit limited internal confirmation (duo-confirmation and the single-name assumed referent). If the summaries do not unambiguously support a single minimal answer and you lack permitted internal justification, emit a concise clarification request as the answer.

Additional specific, evidence-directed selection rules (apply these only when their trigger conditions are met, otherwise follow the rules above):

8) Prefer an explicitly named immediate jurisdiction when resolving county/country wording conflicts
- Trigger: The question asks which country/sovereign-state a county or borough is part of (phrasing like 'county' and 'part of what country' or equivalent) and the summaries disagree or one summary uses a parenthetical membership note such as 'A (part of B)'.
- Behavior: If one summary contains a parenthetical of the form 'A (part of B)', treat the pre-parenthetical name A as the immediate administrative region referenced by the summaries and emit A (trimmed of surrounding punctuation/whitespace) as the minimal answer. More generally, when summaries disagree about a place, prefer the summary that directly names the immediate jurisdiction associated with the county (the subnational or constituent area named in the summary) and extract that short named phrase verbatim as the answer. Do not add any extra words or punctuation.
- Fallback: If neither summary clearly names an immediate jurisdiction or both are equally ambiguous, emit a concise clarification question per the base rules rather than guessing.

9) Size-answer simplification when both summaries agree with an inclusive qualifier
- Trigger: The question explicitly asks for a size (phrasing like 'what size' or 'equal to or smaller than what size' or equivalent) AND summary_1 and summary_2 are identical strings for agreement-detection (case-insensitive) AND that common string matches the pattern '<size> or smaller' or '<size> or less' (case-insensitive, allowing optional surrounding punctuation/whitespace).
- Behavior: Produce as the minimal answer only the '<size>' portion of the agreed common phrase by removing the trailing inclusive qualifier 'or smaller' or 'or less' and any immediately adjacent punctuation/extra whitespace. Emit the remaining size token (number plus unit) verbatim as shown in the summaries (preserve digits, unit spelling/capitalization, and internal spacing).
- Do not apply this edit when the summaries do not exactly agree, when the common phrase does not match the pattern, or when the question is not asking for a size.

10) Prefer identical parenthetical short-names when present in both summaries
- Trigger: Both summary_1 and summary_2 each contain an identical parenthetical short-name substring (a substring of the form '(' ... ')' that, once trimmed, is 1–5 words long, agreement checked case-insensitively).
- Behavior: Extract the parenthetical content (remove the surrounding '(' and ')', trim surrounding whitespace and surrounding punctuation) and treat that extracted short-name as the canonical minimal answer. Emit it verbatim (preserving internal characters and capitalization exactly as shown in the summaries) as the sole content of [[ ## answer ## ]]. Do not emit the longer formal name when such an identical parenthetical is present in both summaries.

Output contract reminder: The produced `answer` must be exactly the minimal answer string determined by the rules above (or a short clarification question when required). The [[ ## answer ## ]] block must contain only that string and nothing else.

response.hop1_query: What was the second album by the singer who was a client of Andros Rodriguez, and whose debut album was  "Santogold"?
hop1_docs: Santigold | Santi White (born September 25, 1976), better known by her stage name Santigold (formerly Santogold), is an American singer and producer.  Her debut album, "Santogold", including the singles "Creator" and "L.E.S. Artistes", received very favorable reviews in 2008.  Her second album, "Master of My Make-Believe", was released in 2012.  In February 2016, she released her third album "99¢".

Santogold (album) | Santogold is the debut studio album by American artist Santigold (real name Santi White, who performed under the moniker Santogold at the time of the album's release).  The album was released on April 29, 2008 in the United States and May 12 in the United Kingdom, on Downtown Records and Atlantic Records, respectively.

Santigold discography | Santi White, better known by her stage name Santigold (formerly Sant"o"gold, a stage name under which most of her material has been released), is an American songwriter, producer, and singer.  White's discography under the name Santogold consists of her self-titled debut album, "Santogold", and four promotional singles, a mixtape, and four music videos.  All of these were released in 2008, except the single "Creator", which was released in late 2007.  "Santogold" peaked at number 74 on the "Billboard" 200, and reached numbers 2 and 6 on "Billboard"' s Dance/Electronic Albums and Independent Albums, respectively.  The album also charted in Belgium, France, the Netherlands, and the United Kingdom.

Andros Rodriguez | Andros Rodriguez is a multi-platinum, Grammy award-winning music producer, engineer and mixer.  His diverse catalog of clients include Pharrell, Justin Timberlake, Christina Aguilera, Florence + the Machine, Shakira, Bleachers, Whitney Houston, Cobra Starship, Santigold, Jewel, Lenny Kravitz, Kelly Clarkson, Girls' Generation, Patti Labelle, James Blunt, Ludacris, and Huang Zitao.

Cold Fact | Cold Fact is the debut album from American singer-songwriter Rodriguez.  It was released in the United States on the Sussex label in March 1970.  The album sold very poorly in the United States (Rodriguez was himself an unknown in the US), but managed to sell well in both South Africa and Australia, with Rodriguez touring Australia in 1979.

Red. White. Green. | Red.  White.  Green.  is the second studio album by American deathcore band Upon a Burning Body.  The album was released on March 26, 2012 through Sumerian Records.  The album was produced by Will Putney, who produced their debut album, "The World Is Ours".  All of the songs on the album are titled after films that Robert Rodriguez produced or directed, continuing a trend set from their debut album.  It is the only album with Jonathan Gonzalez on drums and the last album with Chris "CJ" Johnson on guitar.
My Versions | My Versions is the debut album of Swedish singer Robin Stjernberg who was formerly a member of the boyband What's Up!  with Eric Saade and had placed second in Idol 2011.
response.summary_1: First-hop evidence: passage 1 states "Her second album, 'Master of My Make-Believe'" and passage 4 lists Santigold as a client of Andros Rodriguez. Bridge entity: Santigold (Santi White). Missing fact: the question asks for the singer's second-album title — that title ("Master of My Make-Believe") is already present in the passages. Retrieval directions: 1) Open Santigold's biography/discography page (e.g., the Wikipedia "Santigold" or "Santogold (album)" pages) to confirm the second-album title and release details; 2) Open the Andros Rodriguez client list page to verify Santigold appears there. Clarification: none needed — the singer is identified as Santigold.

2) "Master of My Make-Believe" Santigold album page (Wikipedia or official discography)

His diverse catalog of clients include Pharrell, Justin Timberlake, Christina Aguilera, Florence + the Machine, Shakira, Bleachers, Whitney Houston, Cobra Starship, Santigold, Jewel, Lenny Kravitz, Kelly Clarkson, Girls' Generation, Patti Labelle, James Blunt, Ludacris, and Huang Zitao.

Santigold | Santi White (born September 25, 1976), better known by her stage name Santigold (formerly Santogold), is an American singer and producer.  Her debut album, "Santogold", including the singles "Creator" and "L.E.S. Artistes", received very favorable reviews in 2008.  Her second album, "Master of My Make-Believe", was released in 2012.  In February 2016, she released her third album "99¢".

Santigold discography | Santi White, better known by her stage name Santigold (formerly Sant"o"gold, a stage name under which most of her material has been released), is an American songwriter, producer, and singer.  White's discography under the name Santogold consists of her self-titled debut album, "Santogold", and four promotional singles, a mixtape, and four music videos.  All of these were released in 2008, except the single "Creator", which was released in late 2007.  "Santogold" peaked at number 74 on the "Billboard" 200, and reached numbers 2 and 6 on "Billboard"' s Dance/Electronic Albums and Independent Albums, respectively.  The album also charted in Belgium, France, the Netherlands, and the United Kingdom.

Master of My Make-Believe | Master of My Make-Believe is the second studio album by American recording artist Santigold.  It was released in the United Kingdom on April 24, 2012 and in the United States on May 1, 2012, by Downtown Records and Atlantic Records.  The cover art representing four incarnations of Santigold was designed by Jason Schmidt.  The record was produced with a wide range of different collaborators, songwriters and producers; including her long-time friends of Switch and Diplo, as well as David Andrew Sitek of TV on the Radio and Nick Zinner of the Yeah Yeah Yeahs.

Disparate Youth | "Disparate Youth" is a song from American singer-songwriter Santigold.  The track was released in the United States on 14 February 2012 as the lead single from the artist's second studio album, "Master of My Make-Believe" (2012).
99¢ (Santigold album) | 99¢ is the third studio album by American recording artist Santigold, released on February 26, 2016 on Atlantic Records.

Santogold (album) | Santogold is the debut studio album by American artist Santigold (real name Santi White, who performed under the moniker Santogold at the time of the album's release).  The album was released on April 29, 2008 in the United States and May 12 in the United Kingdom, on Downtown Records and Atlantic Records, respectively.
response.summary_2: Santigold's second album is "Master of My Make-Believe." The provided passages show Santigold (Santi White) is listed as a client of Andros Rodriguez and that "Master of My Make-Believe" was released in 2012 as her second studio album.
